cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out this problem. So, Sally is a girl and has 3 brothers. The question is, how many sisters does Sally have? And each of her brothers has 2 sisters. Hmm. Let's start by visualizing the family. Sally has 3 brothers. So that's 3 boys. Then, each of those brothers has 2 sisters. Because if they're all brothers, they come from the same family. So each brother's sisters would be the same girls in the family. So Sally is one of the sisters. If each brother has 2 sisters, that would mean there are two girls in the family. But Sally is one of them, so the other one would be her sister. Therefore, Sally would have 1 sister. So if there are two girls in total, Sally has one sister. But let me check again. Original statement: Sally has 3 brothers. So the family has at least one girl (Sally) and three boys. Each brother (so each of those three) has two sisters. The sisters they have are Sally and her sister. Therefore, there are two girls in the family. So Sally's sisters would be 1. Therefore, the answer is 1. But I need to be careful here. Let me check once more. If the family has three boys and two girls. The girls are Sally and her sister. So each brother has two sisters: Sally and her sister. So yes, each brother has two sisters. Therefore, Sally has one sister. So the answer should be 1.
